Once we can "speak AI" we're able to do a lot of very useful things that just aren't possible otherwise.

We can emit metrics about AI specific things like token counts.

We can allow or deny traffic based on prompts and responses.

We can circuit break and return a canned response quickly when inference isn't necessary.

We can mutate an outgoing request in ways that conform with things like privacy requirements.

Or even translate from one inference provider's protocol to another's. Meaning we get the same management interface regardless of where our tokens come from.

...
